rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 6, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate the two propositions upon which it bases its motion. First, it failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs have no evidence regarding when Mr. Granados worked in the presence of defendant's employees. Defense counsel's questioning at deposition and Mr. Granados' responses do not show that Mr. Granados does not know "when the plaintiff claims he was in the presence of D. Zelinsky employees allegedly sanding joint compound." Second, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs have no evidence regarding the brand name or manufacturer of the joint/taping mud defendant's employees used in the presence of Mr. Granados. On this point, defendant also failed to demonstrate that if plaintiffs could establish that work was prior to 1978, failure to identify a brand/manufacturer of the mud/joint compound is fatal to their claim that it contained asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
